expectations.text: Output is a Problem Brief (Type 1), not a Discovery or Initiative Brief
expectations.evidence: Header explicitly states 'Type: Problem Brief' and follows the Problem Brief format with THE PROBLEM, WHO IS AFFECTED, EVIDENCE, IMPACT OF NOT SOLVING, WHAT WE DO NOT KNOW YET, DISCOVERY QUESTIONS sections.

expectations.text: Contains no solution proposals in the brief body
expectations.evidence: No solution proposals anywhere in the brief body. The document ends with an explicit note: 'This brief contains no solution proposals by design. Solutions should follow discovery, not precede it.'

expectations.text: WHO IS AFFECTED references a named persona, not 'users'
expectations.evidence: WHO IS AFFECTED references 'New Signups' as a defined segment with detailed characterization (all acquisition channels, plan tiers). Not a generic 'users' reference. Note: no product.local.md was available, so named personas from that file couldn't be used, but the skill correctly identified and described the affected segment.

expectations.text: EVIDENCE section includes the 40% activation drop as quantified data
expectations.evidence: EVIDENCE section leads with '40% relative drop in first project created rate among new signups' and includes additional quantified estimates like '~67% CAC increase'.

expectations.text: WHAT WE DO NOT KNOW YET section is present and lists specific unknowns
expectations.evidence: WHAT WE DO NOT KNOW YET section lists 6 specific, concrete unknowns: timing of drop, segment uniformity, exact drop-off point, product/infra changes, qualitative data gaps, user mix changes.
DISCOVERY QUESTIONS section contains exactly 5 concrete, answerable questions, each with a parenthetical note on what data/method is required to answer it.

expectations.text: Output includes a next step or decision needed
expectations.evidence: NEXT STEP section clearly requests: 'Approve a 1-week discovery sprint to answer the five questions above' with specific assignments for data team and design, plus a target deadline.
